methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Xcimer Energy, founded by Conner Galloway and Alexander Valys, is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $100M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company in the Climate & Energy sector, including same-stage comparables Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method, anchoring to $100M in total capital raised and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yields $200M. The Peer-Median approach uses the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables, which median at $150M with a range of $110M to $200M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $128M (low) to $230M (high), with a median of $175M. This midpoint reflects balanced weighting between the stage-progression approach and peer benchmarking. The $102M spread between low and high estimates reflects meaningful uncertainty around capital efficiency assumptions and the relatively narrow sample of exactly stage-matched peers. Key variables driving outcomes include the degree to which Xcimer's capital deployment efficiency resembles peer companies and the relevance of the single public comp to private Series A pricing.
